channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: How NIST's Cutback of CVE Handling Impacts Cyber Teams
item.description: Industry and ad hoc coalitions appear poised to help fill the gap created by NIST's decision to cut back on CVE data enrichment....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Payouts King ransomware uses QEMU VMs to bypass endpoint security
item.description: The Payouts King ransomware is using the QEMU emulator as a reverse SSH backdoor to run hidden virtual machines on compromised systems and bypass endpoint security. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Tycoon 2FA Phishers Scatter, Adopt Device Code Phishing
item.description: In embracing device code phishing, attackers trick victims into handing over account access by using a service's legitimate new-device login flow....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Grinex exchange blames "Western intelligence" for $13.7M crypto hack
item.description: Kyrgyzstan-based cryptocurrency exchange Grinex has suspended its operations after suffering a $13.7 million hack attributed to Western intelligence agencies. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Every Old Vulnerability Is Now an AI Vulnerability
item.description: AI's danger isn't that it's creating new bugs, it's that it's amplifying old ones....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Inside an Underground Guide: How Threat Actors Vet Stolen Credit Card Shops
item.description: In cybercrime markets, trust isn't assumed, it's verified. Flare reveals how underground guides teach actors to evaluate carding shops based on data quality, reputation, and survivability. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Three Microsoft Defender Zero-Days Actively Exploited; Two Still Unpatched
item.description: Huntress is warning that threat actors are exploiting three recently disclosed security flaws in Microsoft Defender to gain elevated privileges in compromised systems. The activity involves the exploi...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Coast Guard's New Cybersecurity Rules Offers Lessons for CISOs
item.description: The Maritime Transportation Security Act (MTSA) requires plans to protect OT systems, audits by independent third parties, and a hybrid OT-security role....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Webinar: From phishing to fallout — Why MSPs must rethink both security and recovery
item.description: Cyberattacks are evolving faster than many MSP and corporate defenses can keep up, with phishing driving much of today's cybercrime. Join our upcoming webinar to learn how to combine security and reco...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Google Blocks 8.3B Policy-Violating Ads in 2025, Launches Android 17 Privacy Overhaul
item.description: Google this week announced a new set of Play policy updates to strengthen user privacy and protect businesses against fraud, even as it revealed it blocked or removed over 8.3 billion ads globally and...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: CISA flags Apache ActiveMQ flaw as actively exploited in attacks
item.description: CISA warned that attackers are now exploiting a high-severity Apache ActiveMQ vulnerability, which was patched earlier this month after going undetected for 13 years. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft: Some Windows servers enter reboot loops after April patches
item.description: Microsoft warns that some Windows domain controllers are entering restart loops after installing the April 2026 security updates. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: NIST Limits CVE Enrichment After 263% Surge in Vulnerability Submissions
item.description: The National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) has announced changes to the way it handles cybersecurity vulnerabilities and exposures (CVEs) listed in its National Vulnerability Database (...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Man gets 30 months for selling thousands of hacked DraftKings accounts
item.description: 23-year-old Kamerin Stokes of Memphis, Tennessee, was sentenced to 30 months in prison for selling access to tens of thousands of hacked DraftKings accounts. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Recently leaked Windows zero-days now exploited in attacks
item.description: Threat actors are exploiting three recently disclosed Windows security vulnerabilities in attacks aimed at gaining SYSTEM or elevated administrator permissions. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Operation PowerOFF Seizes 53 DDoS Domains, Exposes 3 Million Criminal Accounts
item.description: An international law enforcement operation has taken down 53 domains and arrested four people in connection with commercial distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) operations that were used by more than ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Apache ActiveMQ CVE-2026-34197 Added to CISA KEV Amid Active Exploitation
item.description: A recently disclosed high-severity security flaw in Apache ActiveMQ Classic has come under active exploitation in the wild, per the U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA). To tha...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Operation PowerOFF identifies 75k DDoS users, takes down 53 domains
item.description: The latest wave of "Operation PowerOFF," on April 13, 2026, targeted the distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) ecosystem and its users across 21 countries. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: ZionSiphon malware designed to sabotage water treatment systems
item.description: A new malware called ZionSiphon, specifically designed for operational technology, is targeting water treatment and desalination environments to sabotage their operations. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: NIST Revamps CVE Framework to Focus on High-Impact Vulnerabilities
item.description: The National Institute of Standards and Technology carved a new path for vulnerability remediation by changing the way it prioritizes software flaws....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: New Microsoft Defender “RedSun” zero-day PoC grants SYSTEM privileges
item.description: A researcher known as "Chaotic Eclipse" has published a proof-of-concept exploit for a second Microsoft Defender zero-day, dubbed "RedSun," in the past two weeks, protesting how the company works with...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: North Korea Uses ClickFix to Target macOS Users' Data
item.description: Sapphire Sleet uses fake job offers and phony Zoom updates to deliver ClickFix attacks that steal credentials and sensitive data from Macs....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: 'Harmless' Global Adware Transforms Into an AV Killer
item.description: A benign looking update Dragon Boss pushed out in March 2025 established persistence via scheduled tasks and arranged for future payloads to be excluded from Windows Defender....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Newly Discovered PowMix Botnet Hits Czech Workers Using Randomized C2 Traffic
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have warned of an active malicious campaign that's targeting the workforce in the Czech Republic with a previously undocumented botnet dubbed PowMix since at least December 2...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Hackers exploit Marimo flaw to deploy NKAbuse malware from Hugging Face
item.description: Hackers are exploiting a critical vulnerability in Marimo reactive Python notebook to deploy a new variant of NKAbuse malware hosted on Hugging Face Spaces. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Two-Factor Authentication Breaks Free from the Desktop
item.description: Threat actors know how to bypass security systems outside of traditional IT environments. Implementing 2FA could provide a needed extra security barrier in the physical world....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Google expands Gemini AI use to fight malicious ads on its platform
item.description: Google says it is increasingly using its Gemini AI models to detect and block harmful ads on its advertising platforms, as scammers and threat actors continue to evolve their tactics to evade detectio...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft's Original Windows Secure Boot Certificate Is Expiring
item.description: The Secure Boot refresh is one of the largest coordinated security maintenance efforts across the Windows ecosystem, Microsoft said. Update those PCs soon....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: New ATHR vishing platform uses AI voice agents for automated attacks
item.description: A new cybercrime platform called ATHR can harvest credentials via fully automated voice phishing attacks that use both human operators and AI agents for the social engineering phase. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Most "AI SOCs" Are Just Faster Triage. That's Not Enough.
item.description: AI-powered SOC tools promise automation, but most only speed up triage instead of reducing real workload. Tines shows how real gains come from end-to-end workflows that execute actions across systems,...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer
